UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's profile information within the Bimmer CR application. This entity stores application-specific user details, separate from authentication credentials.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity. This typically correlates with the user's ID from an external authentication system.

firstName.description: The first name of the user.

lastName.description: The last name of the user.

email.description: The email address of the user.

phone.description: The phone number of the user.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the user profile was updated.

Address.title: Address
Address.description: Represents a physical address for a user, which can be used for shipping or billing.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Address entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile this address belongs to. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Address)

addressLine1.description: The first line of the street address (e.g., street name, house number).

addressLine2.description: The second line of the street address (e.g., apartment, suite, building).

stateProvince.description: The state or province of the address.

postalCode.description: The postal code of the address.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the address was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the address was updated.

Product.title: Product
Product.description: Represents a BMW original or aftermarket part available in the Bimmer CR product catalog (Firestore 'repuestos' collection).

id.description: Unique identifier for the Product entity.

name.description: The name of the product (e.g., 'BMW M3 Brake Pad Set').

description.description: A detailed description of the product, including features and specifications.

price.description: The selling price of the product.

currency.description: The currency in which the price is denominated (e.g., 'USD', 'CRC').

categoryIds.description: References to Category entities this product belongs to. (Relationship: Category N:N Product)

modelCompatibility.description: A list of BMW models (e.g., 'E90', 'F30', 'G20') that this product is compatible with.

imageUrls.description: An array of URLs pointing to images of the product.

stockQuantity.description: The number of units of this product currently in stock.

sku.description: Stock Keeping Unit, a unique identifier for the product for inventory management.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the product was added to the catalog.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the product details were updated.

Category.title: Category
Category.description: Represents a classification for products, enabling advanced filtering and organization within the product catalog.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Category entity.

name.description: The name of the category (e.g., 'Engine Parts', 'Brakes', 'Interior').

description.description: A brief description of what the category encompasses.

imageUrl.description: An optional URL to an image representing the category.

parentCategoryId.description: Reference to a parent Category, allowing for hierarchical categories. (Relationship: Category 1:N Category)

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the category was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the category details were updated.

Vehicle.title: Vehicle
Vehicle.description: Represents a used BMW vehicle listed for sale in the Bimmer CR application (Firestore 'vehiculos' collection).

id.description: Unique identifier for the Vehicle entity.

year.description: The manufacturing year of the vehicle.

price.description: The selling price of the vehicle.

currency.description: The currency in which the price is denominated.

mileage.description: The current mileage of the vehicle.

exteriorColor.description: The exterior color of the vehicle.

interiorColor.description: The interior color of the vehicle.

features.description: A list of notable features included with the vehicle (e.g., 'Sunroof', 'Heated Seats').

vin.description: The Vehicle Identification Number (VIN), which is a unique identifier for the vehicle.

imageUrls.description: An array of URLs pointing to images of the vehicle.

description.description: A detailed description of the vehicle, its history, and condition.

location.description: The geographical location where the vehicle is available (e.g., 'San Jose, Costa Rica').

status.description: The current status of the vehicle listing (e.g., 'Available', 'Sold', 'Pending').

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the vehicle was listed.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the vehicle details were updated.

Appointment.title: Appointment
Appointment.description: Represents a scheduled inspection appointment for a vehicle (Firestore 'citas' collection).

id.description: Unique identifier for the Appointment entity.

vehicleId.description: Reference to the Vehicle for which the appointment is scheduled. (Relationship: Vehicle 1:N Appointment)

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who scheduled the appointment. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Appointment)

appointmentDateTime.description: The selected date and time for the inspection appointment.

notes.description: Any special notes or requests provided by the user for the appointment.

status.description: The current status of the appointment (e.g., 'Scheduled', 'Confirmed', 'Completed', 'Cancelled').

serviceType.description: The type of service requested for the appointment (e.g., 'Vehicle Inspection').

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the appointment was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the appointment details were updated.

ShoppingCart.title: ShoppingCart
ShoppingCart.description: Represents a persistent shopping cart for a user, holding items selected for purchase.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ShoppingCart entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who owns this shopping cart. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:1 ShoppingCart)

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the shopping cart was first created for the user.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the shopping cart was modified (e.g., item added/removed).

CartItem.title: CartItem
CartItem.description: Represents a single product item added to a user's shopping cart.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CartItem entity.

cartId.description: Reference to the ShoppingCart this item belongs to. (Relationship: ShoppingCart 1:N CartItem)

productId.description: Reference to the Product entity added to the cart. (Relationship: Product 1:N CartItem)

quantity.description: The quantity of the product added to the cart.

addedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the item was added to the cart.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the cart item was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the cart item was updated.

Order.title: Order
Order.description: Represents a customer order placed through the checkout process (Firestore 'pedidos' collection).

id.description: Unique identifier for the Order entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who placed the order. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Order)

orderDate.description: The date and time when the order was placed.

status.description: The current status of the order (e.g., 'Pending', 'Processing', 'Shipped', 'Delivered', 'Cancelled').

totalAmount.description: The total monetary amount of the order, including all items, shipping, and taxes.

currency.description: The currency of the total order amount.

shippingAddressId.description: Reference to the Address entity used for shipping the order. (Relationship: Address 1:N Order)

billingAddressId.description: Reference to the Address entity used for billing the order. (Relationship: Address 1:N Order)

paymentMethod.description: The method used for payment (e.g., 'Credit Card', 'PayPal', 'Cash on Delivery').

trackingNumber.description: The tracking number for shipped orders, if applicable.

notes.description: Any special instructions or notes for the order.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the order was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the order details were updated.

OrderItem.title: OrderItem
OrderItem.description: Represents a single line item within an Order, detailing the product and quantity purchased.

id.description: Unique identifier for the OrderItem entity.

orderId.description: Reference to the Order this item belongs to. (Relationship: Order 1:N OrderItem)

productId.description: Reference to the Product entity that was ordered. (Relationship: Product 1:N OrderItem)

quantity.description: The quantity of the product purchased in this order item.

unitPrice.description: The price of a single unit of the product at the time the order was placed.

itemTotal.description: The total price for this order item (quantity * unitPrice).

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the order item was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the order item was updated.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. Access restricted to the authenticated owner (`userId`) and administrators. The `userId` is part of the path for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user, correlating with `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: Stores physical addresses for each user. Access restricted to the authenticated owner (`userId`) and administrators. The `userId` is inherited from the parent path for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user to whom this address belongs.

params.name: addressId
params.description: The unique ID of the specific address.

definition.description: Public catalog of BMW original and aftermarket parts. Read access is public for all users. Write access is restricted to administrators.

params.name: productId
params.description: The unique ID of the product.

definition.description: Public catalog categories for products, enabling filtering and organization. Read access is public for all users. Write access is restricted to administrators.

params.name: categoryId
params.description: The unique ID of the product category.

definition.description: Public listings of used BMW vehicles. Read access is public for all users. Write access is restricted to administrators. Includes an explicit `status` field for state modeling.

params.name: vehicleId
params.description: The unique ID of the vehicle listing.

definition.description: Stores a user's persistent shopping cart. Access restricted to the authenticated owner (`userId`) and administrators. `cartId` is expected to be identical to `userId` to enforce a 1:1 relationship.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who owns this shopping cart.

params.name: cartId
params.description: The unique ID of the shopping cart, typically matching `userId`.

definition.description: Individual product items within a user's shopping cart. Access restricted to the authenticated owner (`userId`) and administrators. `userId` and `cartId` are inherited from parent paths for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who owns the shopping cart.

params.name: cartId
params.description: The unique ID of the shopping cart.

params.name: cartItemId
params.description: The unique ID of the item in the cart.

definition.description: Stores orders placed by a user. Access restricted to the authenticated owner (`userId`) and administrators. `userId` is part of the path for authorization independence. References to `shippingAddressId` and `billingAddressId` are expected to point to addresses within the user's `/users/{userId}/addresses` subcollection. Includes an explicit `status` field for state modeling.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who placed the order.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique ID of the order.

definition.description: Individual line items within a user's order. Access restricted to the authenticated owner (`userId`) and administrators. `userId` and `orderId` are inherited from parent paths for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who placed the order.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique ID of the order this item belongs to.

params.name: orderItemId
params.description: The unique ID of the order item.

definition.description: Stores vehicle inspection appointments scheduled by a user. Access restricted to the authenticated owner (`userId`) and administrators. `userId` is part of the path for authorization independence. Includes an explicit `status` field for state modeling.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who scheduled the appointment.

params.name: appointmentId
params.description: The unique ID of the appointment.

definition.description: Dedicated collection for Database Access Control (DBAC). The existence of a document at `/roles_admin/{adminId}` signifies that `adminId` has administrative privileges across the application. Authorization is based on existence over content.

params.name: adminId
params.description: The unique ID of the user granted administrative privileges.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for Bimmer CR is designed to be secure, scalable, and debuggable, strictly adhering to the core design principles and strategy mandates. 

**Authorization Independence:**
This is achieved primarily through path-based ownership and a dedicated admin roles collection. For all user-specific data (e.g., UserProfile, Address, ShoppingCart, CartItem, Order, OrderItem, Appointment), the `userId` is explicitly embedded in the Firestore path (e.g., `/users/{userId}/addresses/{addressId}`). This allows security rules to directly evaluate `request.auth.uid == userId` without needing to perform `get()` operations on parent documents or other collections to ascertain ownership. This strategy ensures atomic operations (transactions/batches) remain secure and simplifies rule debugging significantly as authorization logic is localized to the document being accessed. Similarly, administrative privileges are managed through a top-level `/roles_admin/{adminId}` collection. Rules check for the *existence* of a document at `get(/databases/$(database)/documents/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid))`, an independent check that doesn't rely on complex hierarchical data fetching.

**QAPs (Queries as Authorization Policies):**
The structure inherently supports secure `list` operations.
*   **User-Specific Data:** By structuring private data under `/users/{userId}/...`, a user's query (`db.collection('users').doc(auth.uid).collection('orders').get()`) will naturally be restricted by security rules to only return documents where the `userId` wildcard matches `request.auth.uid`. This prevents unauthorized data leakage through broad queries.
*   **Public Catalog Data:** Collections like `/products`, `/categories`, and `/vehicles` are explicitly designed for public read access. Any query on these collections will return all visible data, which is the intended security posture. There is no hidden data that a rule might block after a query, ensuring that what a query returns is what the user is authorized to see.
*   **Admin Access:** For administrators, rules will permit broader access (e.g., to list all orders across users). This is handled by adding an `|| exists(/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid))` condition to rules. This allows an admin to perform a collection group query (if applicable) or iterate through users to retrieve all orders, while regular users remain restricted to their own data. This avoids duplicating data for different access patterns.

**Structural Segregation:** Each collection maintains a homogeneous security posture. For example, public products are in `/products`, while private user data is under `/users/{userId}/...`. There are no mixed-access documents within a single collection (e.g., public and private posts in the same collection), which simplifies security rules and makes their intent clearer.

**Access Modeling & Consistency:** Path-based ownership (`/users/{userId}/data`) is consistently applied for user-owned entities. Descriptive wildcards like `{userId}`, `{productId}`, and `{vehicleId}` are used for clarity. Denormalized `userId` in paths ensures authorization independence. Explicit `status` fields are used for state modeling across entities like `Vehicle` and `Appointment`.
